strategies.name: 5/11/18 EMA Crossover + RSI(11) Divergence QQQ Swing Trade
strategies.description: A daily chart swing trading strategy for QQQ (and leveraged variants QLD/TQQQ) combining three exponential moving averages (5, 11, 18 EMA) with RSI(11) divergence analysis. Buy when all three EMAs cross upward and RSI confirms momentum. Sell when bearish RSI divergence appears followed by an uptrend line break and EMA curl-down. The 'air gap' concept warns against entering when price is extended far above the 18 EMA. Demonstrated with several months of 2024 QQQ price action bar-by-bar.

entry_signals.condition: 5 EMA crosses above 11 EMA, then both cross above 18 EMA. All three EMAs pointing northeast (positive slope).
entry_signals.value: bullish alignment of all three EMAs

parameters.color_coding: 5 EMA = hot pink, 11 EMA = red, 18 EMA = blue

entry_signals.condition: RSI(11) breaks above recent RSI peaks (above 60-65 zone). RSI rising in the same direction as price confirms genuine bullish momentum. RSI overbought (>80) is bullish, NOT a sell signal.
entry_signals.value: RSI > 60 and rising, breaking above prior RSI peaks

parameters.important: RSI > 80 is BULLISH, not a sell signal

entry_signals.condition: Do NOT enter if price is extended far above the 18 EMA. A large 'air gap' signals elevated pullback risk. Wait for price to consolidate back toward the 18 EMA.
entry_signals.value: price must be within reasonable distance of 18 EMA

parameters.measurement: visual assessment of distance between price and 18 EMA
parameters.suggested_threshold: 5-8% above 18 EMA based on chart examples (not explicitly quantified)
parameters.action: reject entry if air gap is too large

entry_signals.condition: After EMA crossover, wait for: (1) Initial move up, (2) Pullback to test support (look for hammer candle), (3) Continuation bounce — enter on the bounce.
entry_signals.value: pullback to support after crossover, confirmed by hammer candle

parameters.step_2: pullback to test area (hammer candle at support)
parameters.risk_reduction: lower entry price than aggressive method

exit_rules.condition: Bearish RSI divergence (price higher highs, RSI lower highs) followed by price breaking a short-term uptrend line. 5 and 11 EMAs curling downward.
exit_rules.value: divergence + trendline break + EMA curl = exit to cash

parameters.signal: bearish RSI divergence (price HH, RSI LH)
parameters.important: signal alone is NOT enough — must wait for trigger

exit_rules.condition: Below the most recent swing low

parameters.placement: just below the most recent significant swing low

exit_rules.condition: As price advances, raise stop to just below the most recent significant candle low.

parameters.method: manual trailing — move stop up as price advances
parameters.example: raise stop from 392 to 403 as price moves from 409 to 423

risk_management.position_size: not specified — speaker uses QQQ, QLD, or TQQQ based on conviction
risk_management.risk_reward_ratio: not explicitly stated — trailing stop with divergence exit suggests large R multiples on trend moves
additional_rules: Distinguish between SIGNALS (divergence warns) and TRIGGERS (price confirms) — never trade on signals alone
RSI overbought (>80) means strong bullish momentum, NOT a sell signal
RSI in middle zone (40-60) provides little useful information — works best at extremes

filters.air_gap: do NOT enter when price is extended far above the 18 EMA
filters.ema_alignment: all three EMAs must point in the same direction for entry
filters.rsi_direction: RSI must be moving in the same direction as price for genuine move
filters.signal_vs_trigger: divergence alone is a signal — wait for trendline break trigger before exiting

missing_elements: Exact air gap threshold (distance from price to 18 EMA that is 'too far')
When to use QQQ vs QLD vs TQQQ
How to handle conflicting signals (EMA bullish but divergence appearing)
Short-term uptrend line definition for exit trigger
assumptions: RSI period is 11 (explicitly stated, not standard 14)
Daily chart only — no intraday confirmation
Short-term uptrend lines drawn connecting recent lows
Air gap is visual assessment, not quantified
source_quotes: 5 EMA is the fastest — first to react; 11 EMA confirms; 18 EMA defines the primary trend
RSI overbought means strong bullish momentum, not time to sell
Never buy when there's a large air gap between price and the 18 EMA
Always distinguish between signals and triggers — never trade on signals alone
Use the 1-2-3 pullback pattern for lower-risk entries after EMA crossovers

strategy_names: 5/11/18 EMA Crossover + RSI(11) Divergence QQQ Swing Trade
summary.extraction_notes: Detailed bar-by-bar walkthrough with explicit entry/exit rules, stop placement examples, and trailing stop management. The 'air gap' concept and signal-vs-trigger distinction are well-articulated unique elements. Missing quantified air gap threshold and aggregate performance stats. Speaker walked through several months of 2024 QQQ data in real-time.
summary.video_title: RSI + 3 EMA Strategy | Catch QQQ Reversals Early (High-Probability Swing Trading Setup)
summary.primary_trading_style: Daily chart swing trading with EMA trend + RSI divergence exit
summary.automation_feasibility: HIGH — All indicators (EMA, RSI) are standard calculations. EMA crossover and slope detection are trivial. RSI divergence detection requires swing point comparison but is well-defined. The main automation challenge is the 'air gap' threshold (needs quantification) and trendline break detection (could be approximated with price < 5 EMA). Trailing stop logic is straightforward.
summary.key_insight: The three EMAs work as a traffic light system (all green = bullish, all red = bearish), while RSI(11) catches faster turns than the standard RSI(14). The critical insight is that RSI overbought (>80) is bullish momentum, not a sell signal — and the 'air gap' concept provides a visual risk metric for avoiding extended entries.
